title: About Me | David Smith MP

headings: h2: Freedom of Religion or Belief
text_excerpt: About Me | David Smith MP Skip to content Search for: Search Search Join my Newsletter Join my Newsletter Casework & Support Home About Me My Work Surgeries News Contact Home About Me Search for: Search Search Home About Me David Smith | MP for North Northumberland About Me I was delighted to be elected to serve as the new Member of Parliament for North Northumberland on 4th July 2024. It is the greatest honour of my life to have been elected as your MP. We’re all rightly proud of the beauty, heritage and community spirit that we have in Northumberland. It’s now time to ensure that North Northumberland has a future that shines as brightly as our past. My experience prior to entering Parliament was in the Charity Sector, in which I’d worked for the last 20 years. In the last 7 years I led a homelessness charity in the North East, through which fairness, justice, compassion and empowering the vulnerable was at the heart of everything, and these qualities continue to guide me to this day in Parliament. I promise to work with you and to bring all of my experience to the job to help and support you, and champion our beautiful region. My Roles Alongside representing you in North Northumberland, I am honoured to participate in a number of Parliamentary and Governmental functions to support constituents, and the wider UK. Find out more about this work below. In Parliament I champion North Northumberland in Parliament, representing the interests of our beautiful area as much as possible when the house is in session. My Work in Parliament Freedom of Religion or Belief Freedom of Religion or Belief Working within the Foreign, Commonwealth & Development Office, it is a privilege to be able to champion Freedom of Religion or Belief across the UK and the globe. Member Northern Ireland Affairs Committee My first job was as a peace and reconciliation leader in post-Troubles Northern Ireland. I remain deeply committed to a flourishing Northern Ireland and take my work on Parliament’s Northern Ireland committee very seriously. Find out More Vice Chair APPG for Ending Homelessness I led a homelessness charity in the North East before entering parliament, so I’m proud to be a member of the All-Party Parliamentary Group for Ending Homelessness. It is a continuing priority for me to end homelessness in the UK. Find out More Vice Chair APPG for North Korea I support Britain’s international leadership in freedom and human rights. We cannot ignore the terrible acts of the Kim regime, and Britain has a role to play in challenging North Korea .
